work_unit: M-001 + M-002 redo: lint-guard env var safety + distill LLM status differentiation
situation: M-001 and M-002 were salvaged as patches from the stale auto/optimization branch (rejected because 9 commits ahead with outdated base regressing 3 safety guards). M-001 patch did not apply cleanly (hooks/studio-lint-guard.sh had diverged to add Edit support). M-002 patch applied cleanly.
action: M-001: manually adapted to current main. Key adaptations: (1) preserved Edit support (tool_name in Write/Edit) not in original patch, (2) content extraction handles both content and new_string fields, (3) same env-var-based single-python-invocation architecture as original. M-002: clean git apply, no adaptation needed. The distill-patterns.py change adds llm_status field with 5 states: ok, skipped, timeout, empty_response, nonzero_exit, exception.
outcome_evidence: bash -n syntax OK for lint-guard. py_compile OK for distill-patterns.py. Functional tests: (1) valid Python with shell metacharacters passes through correctly, (2) invalid Python (unclosed paren) blocked with PyCompileError, (3) invalid JSON blocked with JSONDecodeError, (4) Write/Edit/non-Write tools correctly filtered, (5) llm_status assignments found at 7 locations in step2_llm_distill.
